qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Default deployment allows unauthenticated server-side JavaScript execution via /api/runscript
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets POST /api/runscript and the default secureEnabled=false authorization fallback, while the advisory targets POST /api/upload in server/api/projects/index.js, which lacks auth middleware and writes attacker-controlled files.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's /api/upload unrestricted file-upload path or its missing-auth file-write root cause.
evidence: The advisory JSON describes FUXA v1.2.7 /api/upload as an unrestricted file upload that allows unauthenticated arbitrary file writes.

The referenced FUXA source at server/api/projects/index.js shows prjApp.post('/api/upload', function (req, res) { ... fs.writeFileSync(filePath, basedata, encoding); }) without secureFnc or an authorization check.

The accepted finding report instead describes POST /api/runscript, where secureFnc is present and exploitation depends on default secureEnabled=false behavior plus verifyGroups() returning admin-equivalent access before Module._compile executes attacker-controlled script.code.
